---
title: "eDNA / Metabarcoding"
---

eDNA and metabarcoding workflows provide a deeper laboratory view of soil biological communities.

| Item | Description |
|---|---|
| **What it is** | A laboratory workflow for analysing biological material present in soil. |
| **Main purpose** | To characterise biodiversity and biological-community composition. |
| **Where used** | Laboratory |
| **Typical users** | Researchers, specialist laboratories |
| **Typical outputs** | Richness, diversity, evenness, and functional-group information |

## Main target variables

- fungal diversity,
- bacterial diversity,
- archaeal diversity,
- and selected functional-group indicators depending on the workflow.

## Descriptor groups supported

- biodiversity,
- biological functioning,
- and advanced ecological interpretation.

## Practical note

This is a more specialised toolbox component and is most useful when users need a deeper view of soil biological communities.
